title: My Love Is In A Light Attire
content:
My love is in a light attire
    Among the apple-trees,
    Where the gay winds do most desire
    To run in companies.

    There, where the gay winds stay to woo
    The young leaves as they pass,
    My love goes slowly, bending to
    Her shadow on the grass;

    And where the sky�s a pale blue cup
    Over the laughing land,
    My love goes lightly, holding up
    Her dress with dainty hand.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Joyce, titled "My Love Is In A Light Attire", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
